action: Open a supported indexed pool workspace, begin a write, and compare the displayed pair with configured-factory lookup and live pair RPC reads before simulation.
actor: Connected liquidity provider on the configured chain

expectedAssertions.statement: Factory lookup resolves the exact displayed pair address for the reviewed token X, token Y, and bin step.
Live pair factory, token order, bin step, and deployment chain equal the manifest and indexed identity.
The review binds the attested pair identity before any simulation or wallet request.
Any foreign, stale, spoofed, or mismatched pair remains read-only with zero simulation and wallet calls.

negativePaths.trigger: Configured-factory lookup resolves a different address than the indexed pool.
Live pair token order, bin step, factory, or chain differs from the reviewed identity.

prerequisites: A configured deployment manifest and RPC are available.
Fixtures include canonical and mismatched indexed pair identities.
recovery: Refresh manifest, indexer, and RPC state.
Select the canonical factory pair and create a new review before retrying.
title: Attest selected pool against configured factory

action: Load hook metadata for a selected pool, verify the indexed hook parameters against live RPC hook address, flags, and code identity, then attempt a pool write.
actor: Connected trader or liquidity provider on the configured chain

expectedAssertions.statement: Hookless pools are identified as hookless consistently by indexer and RPC.
A nonzero hook is writable only when its live identity, flags, code, and declared behavior match an allowlisted entry.
Hook identity and risk are disclosed in the workspace and immutable transaction review.
Unknown, malformed, changed, unsupported, or indexer-mismatched hooks fail closed before simulation or wallet handoff while safe read-only facts remain visible.

negativePaths.trigger: Indexer reports a nonzero hook that is absent from the supported-hook allowlist.
The allowlisted hook code or flags change after indexing or differ from the live pair.

prerequisites: Fixtures include hookless, allowlisted, unknown, and changed-code pools.
Supported hook identities and behavior declarations are configured.
recovery: Refresh the indexed and live hook state.
Resume write access only after the live hook again matches an explicitly supported identity; otherwise continue read-only.
title: Fail closed for unsupported pool hooks

action: Select an empty pool and attempt to request a swap quote, approve spending, simulate, and submit from that selected-pool context.
actor: Connected trader on the configured chain

expectedAssertions.statement: The selected pool is classified as empty before quote or approval readiness is granted.
Quote, approval, simulation, and wallet submission controls remain disabled for the empty selected pool.
No quote-provider, simulation, or wallet call is made from the blocked context.
The user receives an actionable Create Position recovery without any silent switch to another pool.

negativePaths.trigger: Indexed pool reserves or executable liquidity are zero.
A delayed quote response arrives after the pool becomes empty.

prerequisites: An indexed exact pool exists with no executable liquidity.
Quote, simulation, and wallet calls can be counted.
recovery: Follow Create Position for the same pool or select a different explicitly identified nonempty pool.
Acquire a fresh market snapshot and review before enabling execution.
title: Block execution for an empty selected pool

action: Load selected-pool fixtures with missing execution fields, partial source state, or unsafe identity and attempt to progress from quote through wallet submission.
actor: Connected trader on the configured chain

expectedAssertions.statement: Missing reserves, malformed execution fields, source partiality, and unsafe identity each prevent readiness.
The UI distinguishes the blocking reason from a valid zero-value market state.
Blocked contexts produce zero quote, approval, simulation, and wallet calls.
A late stale asynchronous completion cannot restore readiness after a newer generation has classified the pool unsafe.

negativePaths.trigger: The selected pool response omits or malforms a required execution field.
An older valid refresh resolves after a newer partial or unsafe refresh.

prerequisites: Fixtures can independently produce malformed, partial, unsafe, and valid selected-pool snapshots.
Quote and wallet handoffs are observable.
recovery: Start one new generation-owned refresh and create a fresh review only from its complete result.
title: Fail closed for malformed or partial selected-pool state
